events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: troweling bleed water back into the surface is a sequence violation under ACI 302 — finishing (floating/troweling) must wait until bleed water and surface sheen fully evaporate. Doing it early raises the surface water-cement ratio, weakening the wear layer, which is exactly what produced the dusting/scaling seen in the failed-surface photos. Decision: **fail**, risk **high** — stop finishing, let bleed water evaporate on remaining areas, repair or remove the already-defective surface layer, and only then proceed to curing per ACI 308.
